facts_disclosed: Produced pop track for major label, $8,500 fee

turns.content: YO YO YO okay I have NEWS and I literally cannot contain myself rn I've been pacing around the lab for an HOUR
Okay I can feel the energy through the screen! What happened??
so I just produced a track for a MAJOR LABEL artist!! like an actual signed artist on a big label!! I cant say who yet because the NDA is tight but fam THIS IS IT
That's a huge deal! Congratulations! Getting a placement with a major label artist is a massive milestone. How did the opportunity come about — did they reach out to you or did you pitch beats?

okay so basically their A&R heard one of my beats through a producer I collabed with last year. they reached out, asked for a beat pack, loved one of the tracks, and then flew me in for a session to build it out with the artist. the whole ting happened in like 2 weeks I could barely PROCESS it

That's incredible — and that's exactly how the industry works at that level. One connection leads to another and suddenly you're in the room. The fact that they flew you in for a session means they didn't just want the beat, they wanted YOU in the creative process. That's a huge sign of respect.

What genre is the track? Is it in your usual lane?

okay so this is the ting — its a POP track!! like straight up pop. and honestly I was surprised they wanted MY sound for a pop record but the A&R said they wanted something with more TEXTURE and rhythm than the usual pop production

That actually makes perfect sense. Pop production right now is actively pulling in sounds from dancehall, afrobeats, Caribbean music — think about how many mainstream pop hits in the last few years have had those rhythmic elements. Your background gives you something most pop producers don't have naturally.

How did the session go? Was it a different process than what you're used to?

it was WILD honestly. the process is so different from how I usually work. like there were 3 other people in the room — a topliner, the artist, and the A&R just vibing. and the whole thing was way more collaborative than me sitting alone in the lab at 3am. but I put dancehall elements all through the production so it still has SOUL you know?? like the drums have that bounce, there's a little riddim guitar in the chorus, the bass has that Caribbean warmth

That's the best possible outcome — you brought YOUR sound into THEIR world instead of just making a generic pop beat. The dancehall elements are what got you in the room in the first place, so leaning into that was the right call.

The collaborative process is really different from solo production, yeah. But that's how most major label tracks get made — a room full of people bouncing ideas. Getting comfortable in that environment is a skill in itself and you just proved you can do it.

Did they give you any details on the business side — production credit, royalties, that kind of thing?

YESSS okay so the production fee was $8,500!! EIGHT THOUSAND FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS. plus royalties on the back end!! like actual mechanical and streaming royalties. I almost fell out my chair when they sent the contract over. my manager — well I'm self-managing so I guess I almost fell out my OWN chair reading it lol

$8,500 upfront plus royalties is a really solid deal for a production placement, especially if this is your first major label credit. The upfront fee is great but the royalties are where the real long-term money is — if that track streams well, you could be getting checks for years.

Did you have a lawyer look over the contract before you signed? With major labels the contracts can be tricky — things like publishing splits, producer points, exclusivity clauses.

YES I got a music lawyer to review it. cost me $600 but she caught a few things that needed adjusting — the original contract had a clause that would have given them first refusal on my next 3 productions which is WILD. she got that removed. the final split is fair and I keep my publishing share

Smart move getting the lawyer. That first refusal clause is a classic major label move — they slip it in hoping you won't notice or won't push back. Losing first refusal on your next 3 productions could have locked you up for months. $600 to get that removed probably saved you tens of thousands in future opportunities.

Keeping your publishing share is huge too. That's your long-term income.

So when does the track come out? Or is that still TBD?

they said early next year sometime. labels move SLOW fam. they gotta do the whole rollout — single announcement, pre-save campaign, music video, all of that. I just gotta sit here and wait and not tell anyone WHO it is which is literally killing me lol

Ha, the NDA silence is the hardest part! But when that track drops and your name is in the credits, it's going to open so many doors. One major label credit leads to more — A&Rs talk to each other, and once you're proven in that environment, you'll get more calls.

In the meantime, use this energy. You're clearly in a creative peak right now — channel that into making more beats, more music. Build up your catalog so when the next opportunity comes, you have options ready to go.

trust me the lab has been ON FIRE since I got back from that session. I've made like 8 new beats in the last week. something about being in that room with those people just unlocked a new level in my brain. the riddim is flowing DIFFERENT now

That creative momentum after a big session is real — being around talented people raises your own bar. You start hearing things differently, pushing yourself harder. Ride that wave as long as it lasts and stockpile everything.

Seriously though, congratulations. $8,500 production fee, royalties, major label credit, and you kept your publishing. That's a massive W. When that track drops and you can finally tell people, it's going to be a game-changer.

Go back to the lab and keep that energy going!

BLESS!! I honestly just needed to tell SOMEONE because I'm bursting!! even if I cant say the artist name at least I can scream about the ting in general lol. the track is actually SO catchy too I keep catching myself humming the chorus. okay I'm going back to the lab NOW. thank youuuu!!
Ha, I'm honored to be the person you could scream at about it! When you're humming your own production, you know it's a hit. Go make more of those — the next call is coming. Congratulations again!
